Title: Paris municipal elections: Emmanuel Grégoire and the united left achieve a resounding victory
Subhead: Despite a united front from the entire right-wing and centrist parties – as well as competition on the left with La France Insoumise candidate Sophia Chikirou remaining in the race – the Socialist candidate delivered the united left its most impressive victory in Paris since 2001.

Title: Slovenia PM claims election win as results show neck and neck finish
Subhead: Robert Golob, Slovenia's incumbent liberal prime minister, claimed victory, with more than 99% of votes counted. Suspicion of foreign interference shook the campaign, with authorities probing whether an Israeli intelligence firm was behind secretly recorded videos suggesting alleged graft in Golob's government.

Title: 'The oil shock in the Middle East is a godsend for Russia'
Subhead: By joining Israel in its war against Iran, Donald Trump is handing Vladimir Putin an unexpected economic and strategic reprieve, explains Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

Title: Matthieu Jalibert, fly-half for France: 'I don't feel any sense of revenge'
Subhead: For the first time, Matthieu Jalibert has won the Six Nations with France as a starting player. Before returning to the Top 14 league on Sunday, he spoke to Le Monde about how his role on the national team has evolved.

Title: French municipal elections: Full results in every city, town and village
Subhead: French voters elected more than 34,000 mayors on March 15 and 22. Use our search engine to find the winner in any municipality.

Title: French employees' cigarette breaks cost companies €5 billion a year, study estimates
Subhead: The job search website Jobleads based its estimate on tobacco consumption, the number of employees, the smoking rate, the average length of breaks and salary data. However, the figure should be taken with caution.
